title: SOURCE ERROR PRO AMAN VASISTH SIR TOPIC 7 INVERSION QU

questions.question: So rudely she behaved (a)/ that everybody was (b)/ offended in the party. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "So rudely did she behave".

questions.question: He (a)/ is (b)/ very (c)/ wiser than you. (d)
questions.explanation: Comparative degree ke pehle 'much' lagta hai: "much wiser".

questions.question: They are (a)/ very senior to me (b)/ so I dare not play a joke. (c)/ No error (d)
options: so I dare not play a joke
questions.explanation: 'Senior' ek adjective hai jiske saath simple comparison mein 'much' ya context ke hisab se change hota hai (PDF solution: much senior).

questions.question: He (a)/ ran (b)/ as fastly (c)/ as he could. (d)
questions.explanation: 'Fastly' koi word nahi hota, 'fast' hi adverb hai.

questions.question: Somebody reported (a)/ that his partner (b)/ had only died (c)/ a week before. (d)
questions.explanation: 'Only' ki position galat hai: "died only a week before".

questions.question: Never in the history (a)/ of India we have found (b)/ such a feeling of nationalism. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "have we found".

questions.question: He could neither clearly identify the man (a)/ nor he could (b)/ name the weapon. (c)/ No error (d)
options: He could neither clearly identify the man
questions.explanation: 'Nor' ke baad inversion: "nor could he".

questions.question: I haven't been (a)/ to New York before and (b)/ neither my sister. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "neither has my sister" (Verb missing hai).

questions.question: I am (a)/ very much sorry (b)/ to learn (c)/ about your loss. (d)
questions.explanation: 'Sorry' ke pehle sirf 'very' kaafi hai.

questions.question: She sang (a)/ very (b)/ good (c)/ in the party. (d)
questions.explanation: Verb 'sang' ko modify karne ke liye adverb 'well' chahiye.

questions.question: He (a)/ works (b)/ hardly (c)/ to pass the exam. (d)
questions.explanation: Mehnat ke liye "works hard".

questions.question: Not long ago (a)/ did Ruchika come (b)/ across a snake. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Not long ago' ke saath inversion zaroori nahi hai: "Ruchika came".

questions.question: When asked (a)/ he took tea (b)/ and said it tasted sweetly. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Taste' linking verb hai: "tasted sweet".

questions.question: No sooner the government enacted (a)/ the act than the condition (b)/ started getting better. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "No sooner did the government enact".

questions.question: I don't like (a)/ such nasty things, (b)/ nor he does. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "nor does he".

questions.question: He is (a)/ very miser (b)/ and cannot (c)/ spend a single penny. (d)
questions.explanation: 'Miser' noun hai, adjective 'miserly' chahiye: "very miserly".

questions.question: This room is (a)/ very beautiful (b)/ but too much small. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Adjective ke pehle 'much too' aata hai: "much too small".

questions.question: It (a)/ is (b)/ raining (c)/ hardly. (d)
questions.explanation: Tezz baarish ke liye "raining hard".

questions.question: He (a)/ looks (b)/ sadly (c)/ today. (d)
questions.explanation: Linking verb 'looks' ke baad adjective 'sad' aayega.

questions.question: Seldom if ever (a)/ nature does operate (b)/ in closed and separate. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Seldom' negative adverb hai: "does nature operate".

questions.question: Little he realized (a)/ that he was (b)/ being laughed at. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Little' negative sense mein inversion leta hai: "Little did he realize".

questions.question: I haven't been to any (a)/ European country and neither (b)/ my friends have. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Neither ke baad inversion: "have my friends".

questions.question: Never I have listened (a)/ to such beautiful music (b)/ as the piece we heard last night. (c)/ No error (d)
options: as the piece we heard last night
questions.explanation: 'Never' ke saath inversion: "Never have I listened".

questions.question: So well Suman sang (a)/ that everyone (b)/ started clapping in the class. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Jab sentence 'So + Adverb of manner' se shuru ho, toh inversion hota hai: "So well did Suman sing".

questions.question: Who will believe (a)/ that she was not (b)/ hardly hit (c)/ by her husband's death? (d)
questions.explanation: 'Not' aur 'Hardly' dono negative hain, saath nahi aayenge.

questions.question: No sooner (a)/ they had received the guests (b)/ than they began entertaining them. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Sahi roop: "had they received".

questions.question: Only when you have your own (a)/ children you will understand (b)/ how difficult it is. (c)/ No error (d)
options: Only when you have your own
questions.explanation: Main clause mein inversion: "will you understand".

questions.question: To such an extent (a)/ the bike was damaged (b)/ that it couldn't be repaired. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Sahi: "was the bike damaged".

questions.question: There is (a)/ much too (b)/ water (c)/ in the tank. (d)
questions.explanation: Noun (water) ke pehle 'too much' aata hai.

questions.question: Seldom (a)/ he (b)/ comes (c)/ to see me. (d)
questions.explanation: Sentence 'Seldom' se shuru hone par inversion: "Seldom does he come".

questions.question: Hardly I had (a)/ seen the dog (b)/ when it attacked me. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Hardly' ek negative adverb hai, iske baad inversion mandatory hai. Sahi: "Hardly had I".

questions.question: I wasn't (a)/ at the last meeting and (b)/ neither was you. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'You' ke saath plural verb: "neither were you".

questions.question: Never I asked (a)/ my Englishman (b)/ how much he earned. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Past tense mein: "Never did I ask".

questions.question: Not only Mohit criticized (a)/ the politician but also (b)/ made some derisive remarks. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Not only' se shuru hone par inversion hota hai. Sahi: "Not only did Mohit criticize".

questions.question: He is (a)/ enough rich (b)/ to buy (c)/ a car. (d)
questions.explanation: "rich enough".

questions.question: No sooner Ankit had realized (a)/ his blunder than he began (b)/ to take corrective measures. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'No sooner' hamesha inversion leta hai: "No sooner had Ankit realized".

questions.question: She is not an engineer, (a)/ nor she is (b)/ a doctor. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Nor' se pehle inversion: "nor is she".

questions.question: The old man is blindly (a)/ so don't call him (b)/ a liar. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Linking verb 'is' ke baad adjective 'blind' chahiye.

questions.question: So rudely Mohit spoke (a)/ that Antim felt (b)/ extremely offended. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "So rudely did Mohit speak".

questions.question: Not only the farmers lost (a)/ what they had on their disposal, (b)/ but they also lost their patience. (c)/ No error (d)
options: what they had on their disposal
but they also lost their patience
questions.explanation: Sahi inversion: "Not only did the farmers lose".

questions.question: So well the work was (a)/ done that I could not (b)/ point out a single mistake. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "So well was the work done".

questions.question: Under no circumstances (a)/ he can help (b)/ us in this illegal work. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Inversion mandatory hai: "can he help".

questions.question: I met him (a)/ often (b)/ near (c)/ the station. (d)
questions.explanation: Adverb of frequency main verb se pehle: "I often met him".

questions.question: So well Karan organized (a)/ the party that (b)/ everyone was spell-bounded. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "So well did Karan organize".

questions.question: Not a word (a)/ they spoke (b)/ to the unfortunate wife. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "did they speak".

questions.question: He (a)/ comes (b)/ seldom (c)/ to my house. (d)
questions.explanation: "He seldom comes".

questions.question: Under a tree (a)/ the saint was sitting (b)/ whom we had seen. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Adverb of place se shuru hone par verb subject se pehle: "was sitting the saint".

questions.question: Under no circumstances (a)/ did he prosper (b)/ as a consultant. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: PDF solution ke anusar tense check: "has he prospered" (since time ke liye).

questions.question: She is (a)/ enough kind (b)/ to help (c)/ everybody. (d)
questions.explanation: Enough adjective ke baad aata hai: "kind enough".

questions.question: Hardly Rohit knows (a)/ anything about the economical (b)/ condition of his state. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: "Hardly does Rohit know".
